one_time_description: Treaty land entitlement settlement of $150,000,000 from Government of Canada ratified August 13, 2019; member distributions of $42,205,019 paid out during the year
economic_notes: First Nation received $150M land treaty settlement. Has investments in forestry, energy, sawmill and power generation (NSILP), community economic development (WDLP), and camp operations (Island View). CIBC revolving credit facility of $90M established for economic development and social programming. Legacy trust account established post-year-end. Castle Homes & Building Products wound up post-year-end.
notes: Major one-time event: $150M treaty land settlement from Government of Canada recorded as receivable at March 31, 2020; funds received June 2, 2020 post-year-end. Member distributions of $42.2M paid. $50M CIBC bridge loan taken to fund distributions pending receipt of settlement. Land settlement process receivable of $150M and corresponding long-term debt of $50M significantly distort balance sheet. Net financial assets swung from -$5.2M to +$101.4M due to settlement. Segmented revenue total of $153,182,374 includes deferred revenue adjustments; consolidated statement of operations shows same total. Prior year comparative expenses restated to $3,708,999 in segmented note vs $3,289,113 on face of statement — difference may relate to accounting policy change for GBEs. No schedule of remuneration disclosed. Province is Ontario (Sault Ste. Marie auditor, ON-based operations). Missanabie Cree Development Corporation fully consolidated; six GBEs on modified equity basis.
